reasoning: The current ACLED reference value for protests in Sint Maarten is 1.000, which mathematically implies there have been 0 recorded protests over the last 360 days (since the formula is 1 + (30-day average over 360 days), meaning an average of exactly 0 protests per 30-day period). To resolve exactly, there must be 11 or more protests in the 30 days before any of the resolution dates. Sint Maarten is extremely small, with a population near 42,000, and my searches for ongoing unrest, protests, or strikes yielded almost nothing relevant for late 2024 or early 2025 except a few labor or infrastructural issues (e.g. at GEBE) that haven't triggered widespread, continuous daily protests. An environment that produces 11 discrete protest events in 30 days would signify deep political crisis or a general strike where different gatherings are distinctively coded. Given the baseline of absolutely 0 events in the past year, generating more than 10 within a single month is incredibly unlikely. I assign 2% probability to near-term dates and 3% to dates further out, simply to account for minor probability expansion over time due to unknown future tail-events.
